title: JV-12 - Routing and no-silent-cloud fallback contract for local MT

labels.name: priority:P0
labels.description: Must-have for the first usable product increment

labels.name: level: atomic
labels.description: Small independent execution slice that can be assigned directly

labels.name: area: privacy
labels.description: Privacy, retention, consent, and local data protection

body:
## Summary

Wire local MT route resolution into runtime provider selection and preserve explicit cloud fallback consent.

## Why is this needed?

Key presence alone must never send meeting text to Google. Unsupported pairs and local failures need visible handling.

## Current context

This WBS comes from tentacle research on 2026-05-21. Current repo state: mt_provider still defaults to google; src/bin/mt_bench.rs emits a pending lf-04-v1 fixture; local OPUS-MT provider exists behind local-mt but lacks benchmark-proven realtime replacement evidence; config has explicit mt_cloud_fallback consent; #372 closed with routing/fixture only; #383 tracks 60fps render performance; PR #407 aligns docs with local-first STT but does not make local MT default.

## Inputs

- src/providers/mt/routing.rs.
- config mt_cloud_fallback validation.
- RuntimeMtProvider and pipeline MT call path.
- Security/privacy requirements.

## Outputs

- Runtime route resolution for LocalDirect, Unsupported, and explicit CloudFallback.
- Status labels surfaced to TUI/pipeline.
- Tests proving no silent Google call without mt_cloud_fallback consent.

## Implementation approach

- Resolve LanguagePair at provider construction or per request.
- Cloud fallback only when mt_cloud_fallback='google' and key is configured.
- Timeout/local runtime failure should not silently use Google unless a separate explicit policy is designed and accepted.
- Surface user-visible error or source-text fallback for unsupported/no consent cases.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] ja-JP -> vi routes to LocalDirect when local MT selected and model ready.
- [ ] Unsupported pair with no mt_cloud_fallback produces visible InvalidInput and no Google request.
- [ ] Unsupported pair with mt_cloud_fallback='google' uses Google and displays routed-via-Google status.
- [ ] Tests fail if key presence alone enables cloud MT.

## Test cases / evidence gates

- [ ] Routing unit tests for BCP-47 normalization.
- [ ] Mock Google provider call-count tests.
- [ ] Config validation tests for mt_cloud_fallback.
- [ ] TUI/status snapshot or focused test for route labels.

## Dependencies

- Depends on JV-11 for real local provider and existing routing from #372.
- Blocks JV-13 and JV-15.

## Agent routing

Build: provider/config specialist. Review: tui-security-auditor and tui-rust-code-reviewer.

## Project / WBS

- WBS key: JV-12
- Wave: P3 routing
- Project target: magicpro97/project/2
- Labels: type: feature, area: providers, area: config, area: privacy, area: reliability, priority:P0, level: atomic

## Duplicate / extension note

Extends #372 routing helper into actual runtime behavior.
